title: Task Planning
steps: Review current state: `tasks_list({ status: 'in_progress' })` then `tasks_list({ status: 'todo' })` then `tasks_list({ status: 'review' })`
Search for existing tasks in the area: `tasks_search(initiative)` to avoid duplicates
Understand code scope: `code_search(area)` to estimate which files/modules are affected
Check prior context: `notes_search(area)` for decisions or constraints that affect planning
Check for established procedures: `skills_recall(workType)` to find workflows the team should follow
Create tasks with `tasks_create`: clear title, description with acceptance criteria, priority (low/medium/high/critical), relevant tags
Establish relationships with `tasks_link`: `subtask_of` for breakdown, `blocks` for dependencies, `related_to` for loose connections
Connect tasks to code: `tasks_create_link` to affected files, doc sections, or knowledge notes
Set initial status: `tasks_move` — `backlog` for future work, `todo` for next up
Verify no orphan blockers: `tasks_find_linked` on key dependencies to confirm nothing is missed
Capture planning rationale: `notes_create` for sprint goals, priority decisions, or scope trade-offs
inputHints: Priority constraints or deadlines if any
